<insights>

- The schema files (v1.ts, data-files.ts) are foundational types used across the whole package but are grouped here alongside feature-specific analytics; extracting schema into its own zone would reduce coupling pressure on consumers.
- The MCP server (cli/mcp.ts) being here alongside domain analytics rather than in the CLI zone blurs the boundary between protocol surface and business logic — consider co-locating MCP with other CLI entry points.
- The single test helper file (tests/unit/analyzers/zones-helpers.ts) does not justify a '-tests' label; the zone is correctly named after its production purpose.
- Zone "branch-work-analytics" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- Cohesion of 0.57 reflects a zone that bundles schema primitives, analytics features, and protocol surface together; splitting schema types into their own zone would raise cohesion and simplify downstream imports.
- cli/mcp.ts sitting in this analytics zone rather than the CLI zone creates an unexpected entry point; aligning MCP registration with other CLI commands would improve navigability.
- branch-work-analytics (unit) imports 2 symbols from the e2e test zone — production code must never depend on test infrastructure; this is a layering violation that could cause test code to ship in production builds
- branch-work-analytics → e2e: 2 imports cross the production/test boundary in the wrong direction; production zone must not import from e2e test zone
- branch-work-analytics is imported by analyzers-3 (zone-detection-engine) via 3 crossings — a lower-level analyzer zone depending upward into a higher-level consumer zone is a dependency inversion that breaks the expected layering
- branch-work-collector.ts is placed in cli-infrastructure despite its name indicating a domain-level data-collection concern; if it collects raw branch data for analytics it belongs here, not in CLI
- branch-work-collector.ts is homed in cli-infrastructure but its semantics are domain-level (collecting branch work data for analytics); the file is likely misplaced and belongs in branch-work-analytics, causing CLI infrastructure to own domain logic
- src/cli/commands/workspace.ts is a CLI command file homed in the analytics zone while all other CLI command files live in cli-infrastructure — this single misplaced file is a likely source of several back-imports from analytics into CLI and a direct cause of the bidirectional coupling between the two zones
- pr-markdown-template.ts is a rendering artifact for the pr-markdown.ts CLI command (which lives in cli-infrastructure) but resides here — keeping template and command in separate zones forces a coupling edge that would be eliminated by co-locating the template with its command in cli-infrastructure
- This zone has the worst combined risk profile in the package: cohesion 0.57 paired with coupling 0.43 makes it the most fragile production zone — it is simultaneously under-cohesive (mixed concerns) and over-coupled (dependencies in both directions)
- Move src/cli/commands/workspace.ts to cli-infrastructure alongside all other CLI command files — its presence in analytics is the root cause of back-imports from analytics into CLI and is responsible for the bidirectional coupling pair
- Move pr-markdown-template.ts to cli-infrastructure alongside pr-markdown.ts — co-locating template with command removes one coupling edge between analytics and CLI without any behavioral change
- Flag this zone as high-fragility: cohesion 0.57 + coupling 0.43 is the worst combined score in the package — any PR touching files in this zone has elevated risk of cascading breakage and should require additional review
- [call graph] 1315 internal calls, 268 outgoing, 575 incoming (cohesion: 0.83, coupling: 0.17)

</insights>
